# System Architecture Documentation ## ChronicCareAIPlatform - Comprehensive C4 Architecture Documentation --- ## 1. Architecture Overview ### 1.1 Architecture Design Philosophy The **ChronicCareAIPlatform** is architected following a set of core design principles that prioritize scalability, security, and maintainability in a healthcare context: | Design Principle | Implementation Approach | |------------------|------------------------| | **Interface-Driven Development** | All inter-service communication is defined through explicit API contracts (-api modules), ensuring type safety and loose coupling | | **Defense in Depth** | Multi-layered security with gateway-level JWT validation, service-level RBAC enforcement, and data-level encryption | | **Domain-Driven Design** | Clear separation of core business domains (Authentication, Account, Health Reports, Doctor Services) from supporting and infrastructure domains | | **Stateless Services** | All business services are stateless, with session state externalized to Redis for horizontal scalability | | **Fail-Fast Security** | Authentication and authorization failures are detected at the earliest possible point (gateway) to minimize resource consumption | The architecture embodies the **microservices architectural style** with a **Backend-for-Frontend (BFF) pattern**, where consumer modules act as thin orchestration layers between external clients and internal provider services. ### 1.2 Core Architecture Patterns ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Architectural Patterns" subgraph "Communication Patterns" P1[Synchronous RPC
Apache Dubbo] P2[API Gateway
Spring Cloud Gateway] P3[Service Discovery
Nacos] end subgraph "Security Patterns" P4[Dual-Token Authentication
Access + Refresh JWT] P5[RBAC Authorization
AOP-based Enforcement] P6[Data Encryption
AES-CBC at Rest] end subgraph "Data Patterns" P7[Repository Pattern
MyBatis-Plus] P8[Distributed Caching
Redis] P9[Optimistic Locking
Version-based Concurrency] end subgraph "Resilience Patterns" P10[Rate Limiting
Sentinel] P11[Token Blacklisting
Redis TTL] P12[Context Propagation
ThreadLocal + Headers] end end ``` #### Three-Tier Module Pattern The platform implements a distinctive **three-tier module pattern** for each business domain, providing clear separation of concerns: ```mermaid graph LR subgraph "Module Architecture Pattern" API["API Module (-api)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Dubbo Service Interfaces
• Data Transfer Objects (DTOs)
• Persistent Objects (POs)
• Value Objects (VOs)
• Business Enumerations
• Shared Constants"] CONSUMER["Consumer Module (-consumer)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• REST Controllers
• Security Annotations
• Request Validation
• Response Formatting
• BFF Orchestration"] PROVIDER["Provider Module (-provider)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Dubbo Service Implementations
• Business Logic
• Data Access (Mappers)
• External Service Integration
• Transaction Management"] end CONSUMER -->|"Dubbo RPC"| PROVIDER CONSUMER -.->|"implements"| API PROVIDER -.->|"implements"| API ``` This pattern ensures: - **Contract-First Development**: API modules define the contract before implementation - **Independent Deployability**: Consumer and Provider modules can be scaled independently - **Type-Safe Communication**: Shared DTOs prevent serialization mismatches - **Clear Responsibility Boundaries**: HTTP concerns isolated from business logic ### 1.3 Technology Stack Overview #### Core Runtime Technologies | Category | Technology | Version | Purpose | |----------|------------|---------|---------| | **Language** | Java | 17 | Primary development language with modern features | | **Framework** | Spring Boot | 3.5.9 | Application framework with auto-configuration | | **RPC Framework** | Apache Dubbo | 3.3.0 | High-performance inter-service communication | | **API Gateway** | Spring Cloud Gateway | 2025.0.1 | Reactive gateway with filter chain | | **Service Discovery** | Alibaba Nacos | 3.1.1 | Service registry and configuration management | | **Rate Limiting** | Alibaba Sentinel | - | Flow control and circuit breaking | #### Data Layer Technologies | Category | Technology | Purpose | |----------|------------|---------| | **Primary Database** | MySQL | Relational data storage for all business entities | | **ORM Framework** | MyBatis-Plus | Enhanced MyBatis with CRUD automation | | **Distributed Cache** | Redis | Token storage, session management, permission caching | | **Object Storage** | Alibaba Cloud OSS | Health report file storage (images, PDFs) | #### Security Technologies | Category | Technology | Purpose | |----------|------------|---------| | **Security Framework** | Spring Security | Authentication and authorization foundation | | **JWT Library** | Nimbus JOSE+JWT | Token generation and validation | | **Password Hashing** | BCrypt | Secure password storage | | **Data Encryption** | AES-CBC | Sensitive field encryption at rest | --- ## 2. System Context ### 2.1 System Positioning and Value The ChronicCareAIPlatform addresses critical challenges in chronic disease management by providing a unified digital platform that connects patients, healthcare providers, and care coordinators. ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Business Value Proposition" V1["For Patients
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Centralized health record management
• Easy report upload and tracking
• Direct communication with care team
• Timely notifications and reminders"] V2["For Doctors
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• AI-assisted schedule optimization
• Comprehensive patient health views
• Streamlined workflow management
• Reduced administrative burden"] V3["For Healthcare Organizations
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Improved care coordination
• Enhanced patient engagement
• Audit trail and compliance
• Scalable infrastructure"] end ``` ### 2.2 User Roles and Scenarios ```mermaid graph LR subgraph "User Ecosystem" PATIENT["Patient
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Role Code: PATIENT
Permissions: patient:read, patient:write"] DOCTOR["Doctor
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Role Code: DOCTOR
Permissions: doctor:read, doctor:write, doctor:all"] FAMILY["Family Member
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Role Code: FAMILY
Permissions: family:read"] ADMIN["System Administrator
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Role Code: ADMIN
Permissions: admin:all"] end subgraph "Key Use Cases" UC1[Upload Health Report] UC2[View Health Records] UC3[Manage Schedule] UC4[Complete Consultation] UC5[Send Notifications] UC6[Manage Accounts] end PATIENT --> UC1 PATIENT --> UC2 DOCTOR --> UC2 DOCTOR --> UC3 DOCTOR --> UC4 FAMILY --> UC2 ADMIN --> UC5 ADMIN --> UC6 ``` #### Detailed User Scenarios | User Role | Primary Scenarios | Access Scope | |-----------|-------------------|--------------| | **Patient** | Upload health reports (images, PDFs, text), view personal health history, receive care notifications | Own records only | | **Doctor** | View assigned patients' reports, manage daily schedules, complete consultations with diagnostic reports, receive AI schedule recommendations | Assigned patients | | **Family Member** | View authorized patient information, receive emergency notifications | Authorized patient records | | **System Administrator** | Manage user accounts, configure roles and permissions, monitor system health | System-wide | ### 2.3 External System Interactions ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "ChronicCareAIPlatform" PLATFORM[Platform Core Services] end subgraph "Cloud Services" OSS["Alibaba Cloud OSS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Protocol: REST API
Purpose: Health report file storage
Integration: AliOSSUtils"] SMS["SMS Gateway
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Protocol: REST API
Purpose: Notifications, verification codes
Integration: SMSServiceImpl"] EMAIL["Email Service
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Protocol: SMTP/REST
Purpose: Email notifications
Integration: EmailServiceImpl"] end subgraph "Infrastructure Services" NACOS["Nacos
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Protocol: Service Discovery
Purpose: Registry & Configuration
Integration: Dubbo Registry"] REDIS["Redis
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Protocol: Redis Protocol
Purpose: Tokens, Sessions, Cache
Integration: Spring Data Redis"] MYSQL["MySQL
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Protocol: JDBC
Purpose: Business Data Storage
Integration: MyBatis-Plus"] SENTINEL["Sentinel
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Protocol: Sentinel SDK
Purpose: Rate Limiting
Integration: Gateway Filter"] end PLATFORM --> OSS PLATFORM --> SMS PLATFORM --> EMAIL PLATFORM --> NACOS PLATFORM --> REDIS PLATFORM --> MYSQL PLATFORM --> SENTINEL ``` ### 2.4 System Boundary Definition ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "System Boundary - Included" direction TB INC1["API Gateway
Request routing, JWT validation, rate limiting"] INC2["Account Management
User registration, login, RBAC"] INC3["Auth Server
JWT generation, validation, refresh"] INC4["Health Report Center
Upload, storage, query, audit"] INC5["Doctor Service
Schedule management, AI recommendations"] INC6["Message System
Push, SMS, email delivery"] INC7["Third-Party Service
OSS, SMS, email adapters"] INC8["Utils Module
Security, encryption, interceptors"] end subgraph "Out of Scope" direction TB EXC1["AI/ML Model Training"] EXC2["Hospital Information Systems (HIS)"] EXC3["Medical Device Integration"] EXC4["Payment & Billing"] EXC5["Video Consultation"] EXC6["Mobile/Web Clients"] EXC7["Analytics Dashboards"] end style INC1 fill:#90EE90 style INC2 fill:#90EE90 style INC3 fill:#90EE90 style INC4 fill:#90EE90 style INC5 fill:#90EE90 style INC6 fill:#90EE90 style INC7 fill:#90EE90 style INC8 fill:#90EE90 style EXC1 fill:#FFB6C1 style EXC2 fill:#FFB6C1 style EXC3 fill:#FFB6C1 style EXC4 fill:#FFB6C1 style EXC5 fill:#FFB6C1 style EXC6 fill:#FFB6C1 style EXC7 fill:#FFB6C1 ``` --- ## 3. Container View ### 3.1 Domain Module Division The platform is organized into eight distinct domains, each with clear responsibilities and boundaries: ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Infrastructure Domains" GW["API Gateway Domain
Importance: 9.0/10
Complexity: 7.0/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• JWT Validation
• Rate Limiting (Sentinel)
• User Context Injection
• CORS Configuration
• Request Routing"] UTILS["Common Utilities Domain
Importance: 8.5/10
Complexity: 6.0/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• UserInfoManager
• Security Annotations
• Data Encryption
• XSS/SQL Filters
• Response Wrappers"] THIRD["Third-Party Integration
Importance: 7.0/10
Complexity: 5.5/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• OSS Service
• SMS Service
• Email Service"] end subgraph "Core Business Domains" AUTH["Authentication Domain
Importance: 9.5/10
Complexity: 8.5/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• JWT Token Management
• Token Validation
• SSO Service
• Refresh Token Handling"] ACC["Account Management
Importance: 9.0/10
Complexity: 7.5/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• User Registration
• Login Verification
• Permission Service
• User Identity"] HEALTH["Health Report Center
Importance: 9.0/10
Complexity: 7.0/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Report Upload
• Report Query
• Audit Workflow
• File Management"] DOC["Doctor Service
Importance: 8.5/10
Complexity: 7.5/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Schedule Management
• AI Recommendations
• Workbench APIs
• Consultation Workflow"] end subgraph "Supporting Domains" MSG["Message System
Importance: 7.5/10
Complexity: 6.5/10
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Push Notifications
• SMS Delivery
• Email Delivery
• Template Management"] end ``` ### 3.2 Domain Module Architecture ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "External Clients" WEB[Web Application] MOBILE[Mobile App] end subgraph "Gateway Layer" GW[API Gateway
Spring Cloud Gateway
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Port: 8080] end subgraph "Consumer Layer (BFF)" AUTH_CON[Auth Consumer
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
REST Controllers
SSO Endpoints] HEALTH_CON[Health Consumer
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Report Upload API
Report Query API] DOC_CON[Doctor Consumer
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Workbench API
Schedule API] MSG_CON[Message Consumer
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Notification API
Inbox API] end subgraph "Provider Layer (Business Logic)" AUTH_PROV[Auth Provider
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
JwtUtils
TokenValidationService] ACC_PROV[Account Provider
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
AccountService
PermissionService
UserIdentityService] HEALTH_PROV[Health Provider
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
HealthReportService
Report Persistence] DOC_PROV[Doctor Provider
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
DoctorWorkbenchService
Schedule Management] MSG_PROV[Message Provider
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
MessageService
SMSService
EmailService] THIRD_PROV[Third-Party Provider
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
OSSService
External Adapters] end subgraph "Data Layer" REDIS[(Redis
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Tokens
Sessions
Blacklist)] MYSQL[(MySQL
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Business Data
User Accounts
Health Reports)] end WEB --> GW MOBILE --> GW GW --> AUTH_CON GW --> HEALTH_CON GW --> DOC_CON GW --> MSG_CON AUTH_CON -->|Dubbo| AUTH_PROV AUTH_CON -->|Dubbo| ACC_PROV HEALTH_CON -->|Dubbo| HEALTH_PROV DOC_CON -->|Dubbo| DOC_PROV MSG_CON -->|Dubbo| MSG_PROV HEALTH_PROV -->|Dubbo| ACC_PROV HEALTH_PROV -->|Dubbo| THIRD_PROV MSG_PROV -->|Dubbo| ACC_PROV MSG_PROV -->|Dubbo| THIRD_PROV AUTH_PROV --> REDIS ACC_PROV --> MYSQL HEALTH_PROV --> MYSQL DOC_PROV --> MYSQL MSG_PROV --> MYSQL ``` ### 3.3 Storage Design #### 3.3.1 Database Schema Architecture ```mermaid erDiagram ACCOUNT ||--o{ ACCOUNT_ROLE : has ACCOUNT_ROLE }o--|| ROLE : references ROLE ||--o{ ROLE_PERMISSION : has ROLE_PERMISSION }o--|| PERMISSION : references ACCOUNT ||--o| DOCTOR : extends ACCOUNT ||--o| PATIENT : extends PATIENT ||--o{ HEALTH_REPORT : uploads DOCTOR ||--o{ HEALTH_REPORT : reviews DOCTOR ||--o{ DOCTOR_SCHEDULE : manages PATIENT ||--o{ DOCTOR_SCHEDULE : participates ACCOUNT ||--o{ MESSAGE : sends ACCOUNT ||--o{ MESSAGE_RECIPIENT : receives MESSAGE ||--o{ MESSAGE_RECIPIENT : has ACCOUNT { bigint account_id PK varchar username UK "unique login identifier" varchar password "BCrypt hashed" varchar phone "AES encrypted" varchar phone_hash UK "SHA-256 for lookup" varchar id_card "AES encrypted" varchar id_card_hash UK "SHA-256 for lookup" varchar email varchar nickname int gender date birthday varchar avatar_url varchar address json ext "extensible attributes" datetime created_at datetime updated_at int deleted "soft delete flag" } DOCTOR { bigint doctor_id PK bigint account_id FK varchar license_number "medical license" varchar specialty bigint department_id varchar title "professional title" text bio } PATIENT { bigint patient_id PK bigint account_id FK bigint attending_doctor_id FK "assigned doctor" varchar medical_record_no text medical_history text allergies text chronic_conditions } HEALTH_REPORT { bigint report_id PK bigint patient_id FK bigint attending_doctor_id FK int report_type "IMAGE/PDF/TEXT" varchar category "blood_test/imaging/etc" varchar title text description varchar file_url "OSS URL" text text_content "for TEXT type" date report_date int status "PENDING/REVIEWED/REJECTED" bigint uploader_id datetime created_at datetime updated_at } DOCTOR_SCHEDULE { bigint schedule_id PK bigint doctor_id FK bigint patient_id FK date schedule_day time start_time time end_time int schedule_category int priority "LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH/URGENT" int status "PENDING/IN_PROGRESS/COMPLETED/CANCELLED" text diagnostic_report text prescription text notes varchar cancel_reason int version "optimistic lock" datetime created_at datetime updated_at } MESSAGE { bigint message_id PK bigint sender_id FK varchar title text content int message_type "SYSTEM/NOTIFICATION/ALERT" int channel "PUSH/SMS/EMAIL" bigint template_id datetime send_time int status "PENDING/SENT/FAILED" } MESSAGE_RECIPIENT { bigint id PK bigint message_id FK bigint recipient_id FK int read_status "UNREAD/READ" datetime read_time } ``` #### 3.3.2 Redis Data Structures ```mermaid graph LR subgraph "Token Storage" RT["Refresh Token
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Key: auth:refresh_token:{userId}:{tokenId}
Value: token metadata
TTL: 7 days"] BL["Token Blacklist
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Key: auth:blacklist:{tokenId}
Value: revocation timestamp
TTL: remaining token validity"] end subgraph "Permission Cache" PC["User Permissions
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Key: auth:permissions:{userId}
Value: Set of permission strings
TTL: 30 minutes"] end subgraph "Rate Limiting" RL["SMS Rate Limit
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Key: rate:sms:{phone}
Value: request count
TTL: 1 minute"] end ``` ### 3.4 Inter-Domain Module Communication ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Communication Matrix" GW_AUTH["Gateway → Auth
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Protocol: Dubbo RPC
Purpose: Token Validation
Strength: 9.5/10"] GW_ACC["Gateway → Account
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Protocol: Dubbo RPC
Purpose: Permission Query
Strength: 7.0/10"] AUTH_ACC["Auth → Account
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Protocol: Dubbo RPC
Purpose: Credential Verification
Strength: 9.0/10"] HEALTH_ACC["Health → Account
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Protocol: Dubbo RPC
Purpose: User Info Lookup
Strength: 8.0/10"] HEALTH_THIRD["Health → Third-Party
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Protocol: Dubbo RPC
Purpose: File Upload
Strength: 8.5/10"] MSG_ACC["Message → Account
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Protocol: Dubbo RPC
Purpose: Recipient Info
Strength: 6.5/10"] MSG_THIRD["Message → Third-Party
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Protocol: Dubbo RPC
Purpose: SMS/Email Delivery
Strength: 8.0/10"] end ``` --- ## 4. Component View ### 4.1 Core Functional Components #### 4.1.1 Authentication & Authorization Components ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Authentication Domain Components" subgraph "JWT Token Management" JWT_UTILS["JwtUtils
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• generateAccessToken()
• generateRefreshToken()
• validateAndParseToken()
• revokeToken()
• addToBlacklist()"] JWT_CONFIG["JwtConfig
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• RSA key pair loading
• Token expiration settings
• Issuer configuration"] end subgraph "Token Validation Service" TOKEN_VAL["TokenValidationServiceImpl
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• validateToken()
• revokeToken()
• checkBlacklist()"] TOKEN_API["TokenValidationAPI
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Dubbo Interface
Exposed to Gateway"] end subgraph "SSO Service" SSO_CTRL["SSOController
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• POST /auth/login
• POST /auth/register
• POST /auth/refresh
• POST /auth/logout"] SSO_SVC["SSOServiceImpl
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• login()
• register()
• refreshToken()
• logout()"] end end SSO_CTRL --> SSO_SVC SSO_SVC --> JWT_UTILS SSO_SVC --> TOKEN_VAL TOKEN_VAL --> JWT_UTILS JWT_UTILS --> JWT_CONFIG ``` #### 4.1.2 Account Management Components ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Account Domain Components" subgraph "Account Service" ACC_SVC["AccountServiceImpl
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• login() - multi-method auth
• register() - with role assignment
• updateUserInfo() - with encryption
• updatePassword() - secure change"] ACC_API["AccountManagementAPI
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Dubbo Interface"] end subgraph "Permission Service" PERM_SVC["PermissionServiceImpl
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• getUserPermission()
• hasPermission()
• expandToPermissions()"] PERM_UTIL["PermissionExpandUtil
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Role → Permission expansion
• ALL → READ + WRITE + ALL"] end subgraph "User Identity Service" IDENTITY_SVC["UserIdentityServiceImpl
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• getDoctorInfo()
• getPatientInfo()
• getDoctorInfoByAccountId()"] end subgraph "Data Models" ACCOUNT_PO["Account PO
with encrypted fields"] DOCTOR_PO["Doctor PO"] PATIENT_PO["Patient PO"] ROLE_PO["Role/Permission POs"] end end ACC_SVC --> ACCOUNT_PO ACC_SVC --> PERM_SVC PERM_SVC --> PERM_UTIL PERM_SVC --> ROLE_PO IDENTITY_SVC --> DOCTOR_PO IDENTITY_SVC --> PATIENT_PO ``` #### 4.1.3 Health Report Center Components ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Health Report Domain Components" subgraph "API Controller" HEALTH_CTRL["HealthReportController
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• uploadReport() - multipart
• queryReportList() - paginated
• getReportDetail() - with ownership"] end subgraph "Report Service" HEALTH_SVC["HealthReportServiceImpl
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• uploadReport() - OSS + persist
• queryReportList() - dynamic query
• getReportDetail() - permission check
• convertToVO() - enrich data"] end subgraph "Data Models" REPORT_PO["HealthReport PO
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• report_type (IMAGE/PDF/TEXT)
• status (PENDING/REVIEWED)
• file_url (OSS URL)"] REPORT_VO["HealthReportVO
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• Enriched with user names
• Display-ready format"] end subgraph "Exception Handling" EXCEPTION["GlobalExceptionHandler
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
• handleBusinessException()
• handleValidationException()

Component Category | Components | Primary Responsibilities | |-------------------|------------|-------------------------| | **Authentication** | JwtUtils, TokenValidationService, SSOService | Token lifecycle, credential verification, session management | | **Authorization** | PermissionService, PermissionCheckAspect | RBAC enforcement, permission expansion, access control | | **User Management** | AccountService, UserIdentityService | User CRUD, profile management, identity lookup | | **Health Records** | HealthReportService, HealthReportController | Report upload, storage, query, audit workflow | | **Doctor Workflow** | DoctorWorkbenchService, DoctorWorkbenchController | Schedule management, AI recommendations, consultation completion | | **Messaging** | MessageService, SMSService, EmailService | Multi-channel delivery, template management, tracking | | **Gateway** | TraceAndJwtAuthFilter, SentinelConfig | Request routing, token validation, rate limiting | | **Utilities** | UserInfoManager, EncryptHandler, Filters | Context propagation, data security, input validation | ### 4.4 Component Interaction Relationships ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Client participant Gateway participant Consumer participant Provider participant Utils participant Database Client->>Gateway: HTTP Request Gateway->>Gateway: TraceAndJwtAuthFilter Gateway->>Provider: Dubbo: validateToken() Provider-->>Gateway: TokenValidationResponse Gateway->>Gateway: Inject X-User-* headers Gateway->>Consumer: Forward request Consumer->>Utils: UserInfoExtractInterceptor.preHandle() Utils->>Utils: UserInfoManager.setUserContext() Consumer->>Utils: @RequireRole check Utils->>Utils: PermissionCheckAspect.checkPermission() Consumer->>Provider: Dubbo: businessMethod() Provider->>Database: Query/Update Database-->>Provider: Result Provider-->>Consumer: Response Consumer->>Utils: UserInfoExtractInterceptor.afterCompletion() Utils->>Utils: UserInfoManager.clearUserContext() Consumer-->>Gateway: Response Gateway-->>Client: HTTP Response ``` --- ## 5. Key Processes ### 5.1 Core Functional Processes #### 5.1.1 User Authentication Flow ```mermaid flowchart TD subgraph Client["Client Application"] A["User submits credentials
(username/phone/ID card + password)"] end subgraph Gateway["API Gateway Domain"] B["TraceAndJwtAuthFilter
Receives login request"] C["Route to auth-consumer
(login endpoints whitelisted)"] end subgraph AuthDomain["Authentication & Authorization Domain"] D["SSOController
Receives login request"] E["SSOServiceImpl
Process authentication"] K["JwtUtils.generateTokenPair()
Create Access Token (30min)
Create Refresh Token (7 days)"] L["Store Refresh Token
in Redis"] end subgraph AccountDomain["Account Management Domain"] F["AccountServiceImpl.login()
Query by username/phone hash/ID hash"] G["Verify BCrypt password"] H["Query AccountRole table
Get user role codes"] I["PermissionServiceImpl
getUserPermission()"] J["PermissionExpandUtil
Expand roles to permissions
(e.g., DOCTOR:READ, DOCTOR:WRITE)"] end A --> B B --> C C --> D D --> E E --> F F --> G G -->|"Valid"| H G -->|"Invalid"| T["Return Authentication Failed"] H --> I I --> J J --> K K --> L L --> U["Return Token Pair + User Info"] ``` **Process Description:** 1. **Request Reception**: Client submits credentials (supports username, phone, or ID card login) 2. **Gateway Routing**: Request is routed to auth-consumer (login endpoints are whitelisted, bypassing JWT validation) 3. **Credential Verification**: AccountService queries user by identifier hash and verifies BCrypt password 4. **Permission Loading**: PermissionService expands role codes to permission strings 5. **Token Generation**: JwtUtils creates access token (30 min) and refresh token (7 days) 6. **Session Storage**: Refresh token is stored in Redis with user association 7. **Response**: Token pair and user info returned to client #### 5.1.2 API Request Authorization Flow ```mermaid flowchart TD subgraph Request["Incoming Request"] A["API Request with Bearer Token"] end subgraph Gateway["API Gateway"] B["Extract Bearer token
from Authorization header"] C["Check whitelist paths"] D["Call TokenValidationAPI
via Dubbo RPC"] E["Inject user context headers"] end subgraph AuthProvider["Auth Provider"] F["TokenValidationServiceImpl"] G["Decode JWT & verify signature"] H["Check token expiration"] I["Check Redis blacklist"] J["Extract user claims"] end subgraph TargetService["Target Microservice"] K["UserInfoExtractInterceptor
preHandle()"] L["Extract X-User-* headers"] M["Populate ThreadLocal
UserInfoManager"] N["PermissionCheckAspect
@RequireRole / @RequirePermission"] O["Business Logic Execution"] P["UserInfoExtractInterceptor
afterCompletion()"] Q["Clear ThreadLocal"] end A --> B B --> C C -->|"Not whitelisted"| D C -->|"Whitelisted"| E D --> F F --> G G --> H H --> I I -->|"Not blacklisted"| J I -->|"Blacklisted"| X["401 Unauthorized"] J --> E E --> K K --> L L --> M M --> N N -->|"Authorized"| O N -->|"Denied"| Y["403 Forbidden"] O --> P P --> Q Q --> Z["Return Response"] ``` #### 5.1.3 Health Report Upload Flow ```mermaid flowchart TD subgraph Patient["Patient Client"] A["Patient initiates report upload
(Image/PDF/Text)"] end subgraph Gateway["API Gateway"] B["JWT Token Validation"] C["Inject User Context Headers"] end subgraph Consumer["Health Report Consumer (BFF)"] D["HealthReportController.uploadReport()"] E["@RequireRole PATIENT
@RequirePermission patient:write"] F["Validate file type whitelist
(PNG, JPG, PDF)"] G["Validate file size
(max 10MB)"] H["Verify patientId matches
current user from JWT"] end subgraph Provider["Health Report Provider"] I["HealthReportServiceImpl.uploadReport()"] J["Validate request parameters"] K{"Report Type?"} end subgraph AccountService["Account Management"] L["UserIdentityAPI.getPatientInfo()"] M["Retrieve attendingDoctorId"] end subgraph ThirdParty["Third-Party Integration"] N["OSSAPI.uploadFile()"] O["Upload to Alibaba Cloud OSS"] P["Return permanent file URL"] end subgraph Persistence["Data Persistence"] Q["Build HealthReport entity"] R["Set status = PENDING
(awaiting review)"] S["Associate with attending doctor"] T["Save to MySQL via MyBatis-Plus"] end A --> B B -->|"Valid"| C B -->|"Invalid"| Z1["401 Unauthorized"] C --> D D --> E E -->|"Authorized"| F E -->|"Denied"| Z2["403 Forbidden"] F -->|"Valid type"| G F -->|"Invalid type"| Z3["400 Invalid File Type"] G -->|"Within limit"| H G -->|"Exceeds limit"| Z4["413 File Too Large"] H -->|"Ownership verified"| I H -->|"Mismatch"| Z5["403 Access Denied"] I --> J J --> L L --> M M --> K K -->|"IMAGE/PDF"| N N --> O O --> P P --> Q K -->|"TEXT"| Q Q --> R R --> S S --> T T --> U["Return Success
with Report ID"] ``` ### 5.2 Technical Processing Workflows #### 5.2.1 Token Refresh Flow ```mermaid flowchart TD subgraph Client["Client Application"] A["Access Token expired
(after 30 minutes)"] B["Submit Refresh Token
POST /auth/refresh"] end subgraph AuthProvider["Auth Provider"] C["JwtUtils.validateRefreshToken()"] D["Decode JWT & verify signature"] E{"Token type = 'refresh'?"} F["Extract tokenId from claims"] G{"Exists in Redis?"} H["Extract userId, roles
from token claims"] end subgraph AccountDomain["Account Management"] I["PermissionServiceImpl
getUserPermission()"] J["Fetch latest permissions
(may have changed)"] end subgraph TokenGeneration["New Token Generation"] K["JwtUtils.generateAccessToken()
New 30-min access token"] L{"Rotate refresh token?"} M["Generate new refresh token"] N["Revoke old refresh token"] O["Store new token in Redis"] end A --> B B --> C C --> D D --> E E -->|"Yes"| F E -->|"No"| X["400 Invalid Token Type"] F --> G G -->|"Exists"| H G -->|"Not found"| Y["401 Token Revoked"] H --> I I --> J J --> K K --> L L -->|"Yes"| M M --> N N --> O O --> P["Return new token pair"] L -->|"No"| Q["Return new access token only"] ``` #### 5.2.2 Doctor Schedule Management Flow ```mermaid flowchart TD subgraph Doctor["Doctor Workbench Client"] A["Doctor requests
schedule recommendations"] B["Doctor queries
existing schedules"] C["Doctor updates
schedule status"] end subgraph Consumer["Doctor Service Consumer"] D["DoctorWorkbenchController"] E["@RequireRole DOCTOR"] F["@RequirePermission doctor:read/write"] G["Override doctorId with
JWT userId (security)"] end subgraph Provider["Doctor Service Provider"] H["DoctorWorkbenchServiceImpl"] I["generateScheduleSuggestion()
(AI Mock Implementation)"] J["querySchedule()
Multi-criteria filter"] K["getScheduleDetail()
With ownership validation"] L["updateScheduleStatus()"] M["completeSchedule()"] N["cancelSchedule()"] end subgraph StateValidation["State Machine Validation"] O{"Current Status?"} P["PENDING"] Q["IN_PROGRESS"] R["Validate state transition"] end subgraph Completion["Schedule Completion"] S{"Has diagnostic report?"} T["Record diagnosis result"] U["Record prescription"] V["Set status = COMPLETED"] end subgraph Persistence["Data Layer"] W["Optimistic Lock Check
(@Version)"] X["Update DoctorSchedule"] Y["Return ScheduleVO"] end A --> D B --> D C --> D D --> E E --> F F --> G G --> H H -->|"AI Suggestion"| I I --> Y H -->|"Query"| J J --> Y H -->|"Update Status"| L L --> O O --> P P -->|"To IN_PROGRESS"| R O --> Q Q -->|"To COMPLETED"| R R --> W H -->|"Complete"| M M --> S S -->|"Yes"| T S -->|"No"| Z1["400 Diagnosis Required"] T --> U U --> V V --> W W -->|"Version Match"| X W -->|"Conflict"| Z2["409 Concurrent Update"] X --> Y ``` ### 5.3 Data Flow Paths ```mermaid graph LR subgraph "Write Path" W1["Client Request"] --> W2["Gateway Validation"] W2 --> W3["Consumer Layer"] W3 --> W4["Provider Layer"] W4 --> W5["MyBatis-Plus"] W5 --> W6["MySQL"] end subgraph "Read Path" R1["Client Request"] --> R2["Gateway Validation"] R2 --> R3["Consumer Layer"] R3 --> R4["Provider Layer"] R4 --> R5["Check Redis Cache"] R5 -->|"Cache Hit"| R6["Return Cached Data"] R5 -->|"Cache Miss"| R7["Query MySQL"] R7 --> R8["Populate Cache"] R8 --> R6 end subgraph "File Upload Path" F1["Multipart Upload"] --> F2["Consumer Validation"] F2 --> F3["Provider Processing"] F3 --> F4["OSS Upload"] F4 --> F5["Store URL in MySQL"] end ``` ### 5.4 Exception Handling Mechanisms ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Exception Hierarchy" BASE["BaseException"] BIZ["BusinessException

Technical Implementation ### 6.1 Core Module Implementation #### 6.1.1 JWT Token Management Implementation ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Token Generation" GEN1["Input: userId, username, roles, permissions"] GEN2["Build JWT Claims
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
sub: userId
type: access/refresh
username: string
roles: array
authorities: array
jti: UUID (for refresh)
iat: current timestamp
exp: expiration timestamp"] GEN3["Sign with RSA Private Key
Algorithm: RS256"] GEN4["Output: Signed JWT String"] GEN1 --> GEN2 --> GEN3 --> GEN4 end subgraph "Token Validation" VAL1["Input: JWT Token String"] VAL2["Parse & Verify Signature
using RSA Public Key"] VAL3["Check Expiration
exp > current time"] VAL4["Check Blacklist
Redis: auth:blacklist:{tokenId}"] VAL5["Extract Claims
userId, roles, authorities"] VAL6["Output: ValidateTokenResponse"] VAL1 --> VAL2 --> VAL3 --> VAL4 --> VAL5 --> VAL6 end subgraph "Token Revocation" REV1["Input: Access Token"] REV2["Extract tokenId and expiration"] REV3["Calculate remaining TTL
TTL = exp - now"] REV4["Add to Redis Blacklist
Key: auth:blacklist:{tokenId}
TTL: remaining validity"] REV5["Delete Refresh Token
Key: auth:refresh_token:{userId}:{tokenId}"] REV1 --> REV2 --> REV3 --> REV4 --> REV5 end ``` **Key Implementation Details:** | Aspect | Implementation | |--------|----------------| | **Signing Algorithm** | RS256 (RSA with SHA-256) | | **Key Management** | RSA key pair loaded from configuration | | **Access Token TTL** | 30 minutes | | **Refresh Token TTL** | 7 days | | **Token ID** | UUID for refresh tokens, enabling individual revocation | | **Blacklist Strategy** | Redis with TTL matching remaining token validity | #### 6.1.2 RBAC Permission Implementation ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Permission Model" ROLE["Role
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
DOCTOR (code: 1)
PATIENT (code: 2)
FAMILY (code: 3)"] ACTION["Action
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
READ (code: 1)
WRITE (code: 2)
ALL (code: 3)"] PERM["Permission String
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Format: {ROLE}:{ACTION}
Example: DOCTOR:READ"] end subgraph "Permission Expansion" EXP1["Input: Role + Action"] EXP2{"Action = ALL?"} EXP3["Expand to:
ROLE:READ
ROLE:WRITE
ROLE:ALL"] EXP4["Single permission:
ROLE:ACTION"] EXP1 --> EXP2 EXP2 -->|"Yes"| EXP3 EXP2 -->|"No"| EXP4 end subgraph "AOP Enforcement" AOP1["@RequireRole annotation"] AOP2["@RequirePermission annotation"] AOP3["PermissionCheckAspect
@Around advice"] AOP4["UserInfoManager.getRoles()"] AOP5["UserInfoManager.getAuthorities()"] AOP6{"Permission Match?"} AOP7["Proceed with method"] AOP8["Throw AccessDeniedException"] AOP1 --> AOP3 AOP2 --> AOP3 AOP3 --> AOP4 AOP3 --> AOP5 AOP4 --> AOP6 AOP5 --> AOP6 AOP6 -->|"Yes"| AOP7 AOP6 -->|"No"| AOP8 end ``` #### 6.1.3 Sensitive Data Encryption Implementation ```mermaid graph LR subgraph "Encryption Process" E1["Plain Text
phone: 13812345678"] E2["AES-CBC Encrypt
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Algorithm: AES/CBC/PKCS5Padding
Key: 16 bytes
IV: 16 bytes"] E3["Base64 Encode"] E4["Encrypted Value
stored in MySQL"] E1 --> E2 --> E3 --> E4 end subgraph "Decryption Process" D1["Encrypted Value
from MySQL"] D2["Base64 Decode"] D3["AES-CBC Decrypt"] D4["Plain Text
phone: 13812345678"] D1 --> D2 --> D3 --> D4 end subgraph "MyBatis Integration" MB1["SensitiveDataEncryptHandler
implements TypeHandler"] MB2["setNonNullParameter()
→ encrypt before insert"] MB3["getNullableResult()
→ decrypt after select"] MB1 --> MB2 MB1 --> MB3 end subgraph "Hash for Lookup" H1["Plain Text"] H2["SHA-256 Hash"] H3["phone_hash column
(indexed for queries)"] H1 --> H2 --> H3 end ``` ### 6.2 Key Algorithm Design #### 6.2.1 Multi-Method Authentication Algorithm ```mermaid flowchart TD A["Login Request
identifier + password"] B{"Identifier Type?"} C["Username Login
Query by username"] D["Phone Login
Hash phone → Query by phone_hash"] E["ID Card Login
Hash ID card → Query by id_card_hash"] F["Account Found?"] G["BCrypt.checkpw()
Verify password hash"] H["Password Valid?"] I["Load Roles & Permissions"] J["Generate Token Pair"] K["Return LoginResponse"] L["Return: Account Not Found"] M["Return: Invalid Password"] A --> B B -->|"Username"| C B -->|"Phone"| D B -->|"ID Card"| E C --> F D --> F E --> F F -->|"Yes"| G F -->|"No"| L G --> H H -->|"Yes"| I H -->|"No"| M I --> J --> K ``` #### 6.2.2 User Context Propagation Algorithm ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Gateway participant Service participant Interceptor participant ThreadLocal participant Business Gateway->>Service: HTTP Request Note over Gateway,Service: Headers:
X-User-Id: 123
X-Username: john
X-User-Roles: DOCTOR
X-User-Authorities: DOCTOR:READ,DOCTOR:WRITE Service->>Interceptor: preHandle() Interceptor->>Interceptor: Extract headers Interceptor->>ThreadLocal: UserInfoManager.setUserContext(userInfo) Note over ThreadLocal: Store in ThreadLocal
for current thread Interceptor->>Business: Continue to controller Business->>ThreadLocal: UserInfoManager.getUserId() ThreadLocal-->>Business: 123 Business->>ThreadLocal: UserInfoManager.getRoles() ThreadLocal-->>Business: ["DOCTOR"] Business-->>Interceptor: Response Interceptor->>ThreadLocal: UserInfoManager.clearUserContext() Note over ThreadLocal: Clear to prevent
memory leak Interceptor-->>Gateway: Response ``` ### 6.3 Data Structure Design #### 6.3.1 JWT Token Structure ```json { "header": { "alg": "RS256", "typ": "JWT" }, "payload": { "sub": "123456", "type": "access", "username": "john_doe", "roles": ["DOCTOR"], "authorities": ["DOCTOR:READ", "DOCTOR:WRITE", "DOCTOR:ALL"], "iat": 1704067200, "exp": 1704069000 }, "signature": "RSA-SHA256 signature" } ``` #### 6.3.2 User Context Structure ```mermaid classDiagram class UserInfoContext { -Long userId -String username -List~String~ roles -List~String~ authorities -String traceId +getUserId() Long +getUsername() String +getRoles() List~String~ +getAuthorities() List~String~ +hasRole(String role) boolean +hasPermission(String permission) boolean } class UserInfoManager { -ThreadLocal~UserInfoContext~ contextHolder +setUserContext(UserInfoContext context) +getUserContext() UserInfoContext +getUserId() Long +getUsername() String +getRoles() List~String~ +getAuthorities() List~String~ +clearUserContext() } UserInfoManager --> UserInfoContext : manages ``` #### 6.3.3 API Response Structure ```mermaid classDiagram class Result~T~ { -int code -String message -T data -long timestamp +success(T data) Result~T~ +success(String message, T data) Result~T~ +error(int code, String message) Result~T~ +error(ToBCodeEnum codeEnum) Result~T~ } class PageUtils { -int totalCount -int pageSize -int totalPage -int currPage -List~T~ list } class BaseResponse { -int code -String message +isSuccess() boolean } ``` ### 6.4 Performance Optimization Strategies #### 6.4.1 Caching Strategy ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Cache Layers" L1["Permission Cache
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Key: auth:permissions:{userId}
TTL: 30 minutes
Purpose: Avoid DB queries for permission checks"] L2["Token Blacklist
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Key: auth:blacklist:{tokenId}
TTL: Remaining token validity
Purpose: Fast revocation check"] L3["Refresh Token Store
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Key: auth:refresh_token:{userId}:{tokenId}
TTL: 7 days
Purpose: Session management"] end subgraph "Cache Invalidation" INV1["Permission Change
→ Delete permission cache"] INV2["Logout
→ Add to blacklist + delete refresh token"] INV3["Password Change
→ Revoke all user tokens"] end ``` #### 6.4.2 Database Optimization | Optimization | Implementation | |--------------|----------------| | **Indexed Lookups** | Hash columns (phone_hash, id_card_hash) for encrypted field queries | | **Pagination** | MyBatis-Plus Page with optimized COUNT queries | | **Optimistic Locking** | @Version annotation for concurrent schedule updates | | **Soft Deletes** | deleted flag instead of physical deletion | | **Connection Pooling** | HikariCP with tuned pool size | #### 6.4.3 Rate Limiting Configuration ```mermaid graph LR subgraph "Sentinel Flow Rules" R1["Login Endpoint
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
QPS Limit: 100
Strategy: Direct"] R2["SMS Sending
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Per Phone: 1/minute
Global: 1000/minute"] R3["File Upload
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Per User: 10/minute
Max Size: 10MB"] R4["General API
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
QPS Limit: 1000
Degrade: 50% error rate"] end ``` --- ## 7. Deployment Architecture ### 7.1 Runtime Environment Requirements | Component | Requirement | Notes | |-----------|-------------|-------| | **JDK** | Java 17+ | Required for Spring Boot 3.x | | **Memory** | 2GB+ per service | Recommended 4GB for production | | **CPU** | 2+ cores per service | Scale based on load | | **Disk** | 50GB+ | For logs and temporary files | | **Network** | Low latency between services | <10ms for Dubbo RPC | ### 7.2 Deployment Topology Structure ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Load Balancer Layer" LB["Nginx / Cloud LB
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SSL Termination
Health Checks
Request Distribution"] end subgraph "Gateway Cluster" GW1["Gateway Instance 1

Architecture Validation and Recommendations ### 8.1 Implementation vs Documentation Alignment | Documented Pattern | Implementation Status | Evidence | |--------------------|----------------------|----------| | Three-tier module pattern (API/Consumer/Provider) | ✅ Fully Implemented | All business modules follow this pattern | | Dual-token authentication | ✅ Fully Implemented | `JwtUtils.generateTokenPair()`, Redis storage | | RBAC with AOP enforcement | ✅ Fully Implemented | `@RequireRole`, `@RequirePermission`, `PermissionCheckAspect` | | User context propagation via headers | ✅ Fully Implemented | `TraceAndJwtAuthFilter`, `UserInfoExtractInterceptor` | | Sensitive data encryption | ✅ Fully Implemented | `SensitiveDataEncryptHandler` with AES-CBC | | Gateway-based JWT validation | ✅ Fully Implemented | `TraceAndJwtAuthFilter` calls `TokenValidationAPI` | | Dubbo RPC for inter-service communication | ✅ Fully Implemented | `@DubboService`, `@DubboReference` annotations | ### 8.2 Identified Gaps and Recommendations | Gap | Description | Recommendation | Priority | |-----|-------------|----------------|----------| | **AI Integration** | Doctor schedule AI recommendations are mocked | Implement actual AI/ML service integration with model serving infrastructure | Medium | | **Async Messaging** | No message queue for async operations | Consider adding RocketMQ/Kafka for event-driven patterns and decoupling | Medium | | **Distributed Tracing** | TraceId propagation exists but no full tracing | Integrate with SkyWalking or Zipkin for end-to-end visibility | High | | **API Documentation** | No OpenAPI/Swagger documentation found | Add springdoc-openapi for API documentation and client generation | High | | **Health Checks** | Basic actuator endpoints only | Implement comprehensive health indicators for dependencies | Medium | | **Circuit Breaking** | Sentinel configured but limited usage | Expand circuit breaker patterns for all external service calls | Medium | ### 8.3 Security Recommendations | Area | Current State | Recommendation | |------|---------------|----------------| | **Token Storage** | Refresh tokens in Redis | Consider encrypted storage or HSM for production | | **Key Management** | RSA keys in configuration | Use external secret management (Vault, AWS Secrets Manager) | | **Audit Logging** | Limited audit trail | Implement comprehensive audit logging for compliance | | **Input Validation** | XSS/SQL filters present | Add request body validation with Bean Validation | | **Rate Limiting** | Sentinel at gateway | Add per-user rate limiting for sensitive operations | --- ## 9. Conclusion The **ChronicCareAIPlatform** demonstrates a well-architected microservices system with: ### Architectural Strengths 1. **Clear Separation of Concerns**: The three-tier module pattern (API/Consumer/Provider) ensures clean boundaries between HTTP handling, business logic, and data access. 2. **Robust Security**: Multi-layered security with JWT authentication, RBAC authorization, AOP-based permission enforcement, and data encryption at rest. 3. **Scalable Design**: Dubbo RPC with Nacos service discovery enables horizontal scaling of individual services. 4. **Maintainable Codebase**: Shared utilities module provides consistent patterns for user context, security annotations, and exception handling. 5. **Production-Ready Infrastructure**: Integration with Sentinel for rate limiting, Redis for caching/sessions, and cloud services for storage and messaging. ### Key Takeaways for Development Teams - Follow the established three-tier module pattern when adding new domains - Use the security annotations (`@RequireRole`, `@RequirePermission`) for all protected endpoints - Leverage `UserInfoManager` for accessing current user context - Apply `SensitiveDataEncryptHandler` for any new sensitive fields - Register new services with Nacos and add appropriate gateway routes ### Future Evolution Path 1. **Short-term**: Add API documentation, enhance distributed tracing, implement comprehensive health checks 2. **Medium-term**: Integrate real AI/ML services, add message queue for async operations 3. **Long-term**: Consider event sourcing for audit requirements, implement CQRS for read-heavy operations The architecture provides a solid foundation for a healthcare management platform serving patients, doctors, and administrators, with clear extension points for future growth.
